items.final_wording: Supports clinician-selected testing based on patient and procedure characteristics and offering pregnancy testing to patients of childbearing potential when the result would alter management.

source.title: Practice Advisory for Preanesthesia Evaluation: An Updated Report by the American Society of Anesthesiologists Task Force on Preanesthesia Evaluation
source.population: Patients of all ages scheduled for elective general anesthesia, regional anesthesia, or moderate or deep sedation.
source.setting: Preanesthesia history, physical examination, selective testing, and consultation for elective procedures.
source.exclusions: Exact for medical-record review, patient interview, directed airway, lung and heart examination, and selective testing including pregnancy testing when results would alter management. It is an older advisory, excludes urgent or emergency care, and does not generate tests or management.

exact_location.heading: Preanesthesia testing and pregnancy testing
exact_location.locator: PDF pages 6-9, testing discussion and Appendix 1 summary

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-selected testing based on patient and procedure characteristics and offering pregnancy testing to patients of childbearing potential when the result would alter management.

items.rationale: The ASA advisory supports documenting pregnancy-status discussion or an offered or reviewed test when the result would alter management; it does not infer pregnancy or require testing automatically.
